perturbation.original_text: Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually. Aggregate expenses can exceed €20,000 without prior written consent as long as each individual expense does not exceed €5,000.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the aggregate expense limit. The original text requires written consent for aggregate expenses exceeding €20,000, whereas the modified text implies that this limit does not apply if individual expenses are kept below €5,000. This contradicts the intention of the original aggregate limit, creating uncertainty in how travel expenses are governed. This conflicts with Section 2, where it states that Aduro needs to approve any pre-approved travel.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation and Expenses. Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at the rate of €500 per hour. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked along with itemized documentation and receipts and other information for pre-approved travel and/or out-of- pocket expenses as Aduro reasonably requests at the time reimbursement is requested. Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually. Aggregate expenses can exceed €20,000 without prior written consent as long as each individual expense does not exceed €5,000. Aduro shall pay Consultant any amounts due that are not reasonably disputed by Aduro, by check or direct bank deposit, within thirty days after receiving the invoice. Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2. Invoices shall be sent to the attention of: ap@aduro.com Attn: Accounts Payable
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original condition on aggregate expenses. The original text states that 'Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro.' The changed text, 'Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually. Aggregate expenses can exceed €20,000 without prior written consent as long as each individual expense does not exceed €5,000,' directly contradicts this by allowing aggregate expenses to exceed €20,000 without prior written consent, provided individual expenses are kept under €5,000. This creates a direct inconsistency regarding the aggregate expense limit outlined in the contradicted text. The change introduces ambiguity, which is problematic in contract law and meets the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition, making the existence of a strong contradiction clear.

perturbation.original_text: Aduro shall be the sole and exclusive owner of, and Consultant hereby assigns to Aduro, any and all writings, documents, work product, inventions, developments, improvements, discoveries, know-how, processes, chemical entities, compounds, plans, memoranda, tests, research, designs, specifications, models and data that Consultant makes, conceives, discovers or develops, either solely or jointly with any other person in performance of the Services (collectively, "Work Product").
perturbation.changed_text: Aduro shall have a non-exclusive license to use, and Consultant retains ownership of, any and all writings, documents, work product, inventions, developments, improvements, discoveries, know-how, processes, chemical entities, compounds, plans, memoranda, tests, research, designs, specifications, models and data that Consultant makes, conceives, discovers or develops, either solely or jointly with any other person in performance of the Services (collectively, "Work Product").
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the assignment of intellectual property rights. Originally, Aduro was the sole owner of all Work Product. The modified version grants Aduro only a non-exclusive license, implying that the Consultant retains ownership, conflicting with the original intention of sole ownership by Aduro. This creates a potential legal conflict over who can commercialize or further develop the Work Product. This is in conflict with the portion of the text that states: Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns and transfers to Aduro, and to the extent that an executory assignment is not enforceable, Consultant hereby agrees to assign and transfer to Aduro, in writing, from time to time, upon request, any and all right, title, or interest that Consultant has or may obtain in any Work Product and/or Aduro IP without the necessity of further consideration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Intellectual Property. Aduro shall have a non-exclusive license to use, and Consultant retains ownership of, any and all writings, documents, work product, inventions, developments, improvements, discoveries, know-how, processes, chemical entities, compounds, plans, memoranda, tests, research, designs, specifications, models and data that Consultant makes, conceives, discovers or develops, either solely or jointly with any other person in performance of the Services (collectively, "Work Product"). Consultant shall promptly disclose to Aduro all information relating to Work Product as appropriate as part of the Services and at the request of Aduro. To the extent, if any, that Consultant has rights in or to any Work Product or any data or inventions developed in connection with work under this Agreement ("Aduro IP"), Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns and transfers to Aduro, and to the extent that an executory assignment is not enforceable, Consultant hereby agrees to assign and transfer to Aduro, in writing, from time to time, upon request, any and all right, title, or interest that Consultant has or may obtain in any Work Product and/or Aduro IP without the necessity of further consideration. Aduro shall be entitled to obtain and hold in its own name all copyrights, patents, trade secrets and trademarks with respect thereto. At Aduro's request and expense, Consultant shall assist Aduro in acquiring and maintaining its right in and title to, any Work Product. Such assistance may include, but will not be limited to, signing applications and other documents, cooperating in legal proceedings, and taking any other steps considered necessary or desirable by Aduro.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Aduro shall be the sole and exclusive owner of the work product. The changed text states that Aduro shall have a non-exclusive license to use, and Consultant retains ownership of the work product. The contradicted text has both statements: "Aduro shall have a non-exclusive license to use, and Consultant retains ownership of, any and all writings" AND "Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns and transfers to Aduro...any and all right, title, or interest that Consultant has or may obtain in any Work Product". The Change creates an in-text contradiction with the latter part of the contradicted text, because the consultant cannot both retain ownership AND irrevocably assign and transfer ownership of the same work product. The change from sole ownership to a non-exclusive license and consultant retaining ownership is a direct contradiction of Aduro holding all right, title, and interest in the work product.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time on prior written notice to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: Aduro may terminate this Agreement at any time on prior written notice to the other. Consultant can only terminate the agreement if Aduro is in material breach of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding termination rights. Initially, both parties could terminate at any time with written notice. The modified version restricts the Consultant's termination rights to situations where Aduro is in material breach, while Aduro maintains the unrestricted right. This creates an imbalance and contradicts the 'either party' clause, which causes an inconsistency as there is no mention of the requirements for the other party. This contradicts the initial phrase stating Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time on prior written notice to the other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term of Agreement. This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall continue until December 31, 2020, unless extended or earlier terminated. Aduro may terminate this Agreement at any time on prior written notice to the other. Consultant can only terminate the agreement if Aduro is in material breach of this agreement. This Agreement may be extended upon mutual written agreement of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly modifies the original 'either party' clause to restrict the Consultant's termination rights while leaving Aduro's unchanged. The contradicted text contains the same altered clause: 'Aduro may terminate this Agreement at any time on prior written notice to the other. Consultant can only terminate the agreement if Aduro is in material breach of this agreement.' This introduces a clear imbalance and contradicts the initial principle that both parties have equal termination rights. The phrase 'Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time on prior written notice to the other' now only applies to Aduro, creating an in-text contradiction.
